ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Mulligatawny Shorba; closest_parent 'soup food product' (FOODON_03311069) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Soups and broths) is the closest parent category; no specific Mulligatawny entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Mulligatawny Shorba or a sufficiently close parent soup concept suitable for clinical coding.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Mulligatawny Shorba is approximately 250 g, typically served as a starter or light meal at an Indian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Mulligatawny Soup — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition), lentil-based spiced soup composite; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lentil soup (FDC ID 172462) for cross-validation.
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) provides the closest South Indian lentil soup composite. Values are adapted for a typical restaurant-style Mulligatawny Shorba with ghee tempering. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy lentil soup (FDC ID 172462) used for cross-validation of macros and sodium. Per-100g values represent a prepared, ready-to-eat soup at approximately 8–10% dry-matter content.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — lentil soup and lentil-based dishes; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mulligatawny Shorba. GI of 30 is estimated from the closest analogous food: lentil soup (GI ~28–32) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 international GI tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021). Lentils are the primary carbohydrate source; black pepper, tamarind, and spices do not materially alter GI. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 16.25) / 100 ≈ 4.9 (based on 250 g serving with 6.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~775 mg per 250 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single 250 g restaurant serving of Mulligatawny Shorba may contribute approximately 775 mg sodium (~34% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and portion size or frequency should be moderated accordingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~30) driven by lentil-based carbohydrates with high dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mulligatawny Shorba has a low glycemic index of approximately 30 and a glycemic load of ~4.9 per serving, making it a suitable soup choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when sodium intake is also monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.7 g per 100 g), high dietary fibre from lentils, and beneficial spices including turmeric and black pepper
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mulligatawny Shorba is relatively low in saturated fat and provides lentil-derived soluble fibre that may support LDL cholesterol management; however, sodium content warrants attention in patients with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mulligatawny Shorba is a traditional South Indian spiced lentil soup whose name derives from the Tamil words 'milagu' (pepper) and 'thanni' (water), literally meaning 'pepper water'. It is prepared by simmering red or yellow lentils with black pepper, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garlic, ginger, tomato, onion, curry leaves, and tamarind, then finishing with a ghee or oil tempering (tadka). The soup has a thin to medium-bodied consistency, a warm golden-amber colour, and a complex flavour profile that is simultaneously tangy from tamarind, earthy from lentils, and pungent from black pepper and spices. In the context of Singapore's Indian restaurant scene, it is commonly served as a starter or light soup course, often appearing on menus as 'Mulligatawny Shorba' to emphasise its refined, restaurant-style presentation. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy soup (~52 kcal per 100 g) with a useful protein contribution from lentils (~2.8 g per 100 g), low saturated fat, and a low glycemic index of approximately 30, making it appropriate for individuals managing blood glucose. The primary clinical consideration is its sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g), which is relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. It is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian, and is halal-compliant when prepared without alcohol-based ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Mulligatawny Soup?
Is Mulligatawny Soup good for diabetics?
What are the ingredients in Mulligatawny Shorba?
Is Mulligatawny Soup high in sodium?
What is the GI of lentil soup?

confidence_reason: No exact ICMR-NIN entry for Mulligatawny Shorba as a named dish exists; values are adapted from the ICMR-NIN South Indian lentil soup composite and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy lentil soup (FDC ID 172462). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database lentil soup data. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source provides an exact named match for this dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) lentil soup composite and cross-validated with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy lentil soup (FDC ID 172462); no exact named entry for Mulligatawny Shorba exists in either source. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a plant-based lentil soup with no animal-derived ingredients in the standard vegetarian preparation; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN plant-food entries. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as no hydrogenated fats are used in traditional preparation. 6. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate based on lentil soup GI values (GI ~28–32) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. 7. Dietary flags: is_vegan set to false because traditional preparation uses ghee; is_dairy_free set to false for the same reason. Vegan variants prepared with oil instead of ghee are possible. 8. Sodium value (~310 mg per 100 g) reflects a typical restaurant-style preparation and may vary significantly between establishments. 9. Chinese local name 胡椒汤 (pepper soup) is the closest conventional Chinese-language descriptor used in Singapore's hawker and restaurant context for pepper-based soups; it is a descriptive translation rather than a loanword transliteration.
